# readme-assert

> Run code blocks in your README as tests

readme-assert extracts fenced code blocks from markdown files and runs them as
tests using Node.js. Special comments are transformed into assertions.

Source: https://github.com/laat/readme-assert
Docs: https://readme-assert.laat.dev

## Install

npm install readme-assert

## Quick Start

Tag code blocks with `test` and add assertion comments:

```javascript test
1 + 1; //=> 2
```

Run: npx readme-assert

## Assertion Syntax

Equality (primitives use assert.strictEqual, objects/arrays use assert.deepStrictEqual):
  expr; //=> value
  expr; // => value
  expr; // → value
  expr; // -> value

Throws:
  expr; // throws /pattern/
  expr; //=> TypeError: message
  expr; //=> TypeError: /regex/
  expr; //=> TypeError

Rejects:
  expr; // rejects /pattern/
  expr; //=> rejects TypeError: message

Console output (preserves the log call, adds assertion):
  console.log(expr); //=> value

Resolves:
  await promise; //=> value
  promise; //=> resolves to value

## Code Block Tags

Supported languages: javascript, js, typescript, ts

Tag blocks as tests:
  ```javascript test
  ```javascript should description
  ```ts test

Group blocks to share scope:
  ```javascript test:groupname

Auto-discover mode (--auto): runs any block with assertion comments.
All mode (--all): runs every JS/TS block.

## CLI

Usage: readme-assert [options]

Options:
  --file, -f        readme file to read
  --main, -m        entry point of the module
  --auto, -a        auto-discover test blocks
  --all, -l         run all supported code blocks
  --require, -r     require a CJS module before running (passed to node --require)
  --import, -i      import an ESM module before running (passed to node --import)
  --print-code, -p  print the transformed code
  --version, -v     show version
  -h, --help        show help

## Import Renaming

Imports of your package name are automatically rewritten to point to local
source. Reads the `name` field from package.json and resolves via `main`,
`exports`, or `--main` override. Sub-path imports and require() are also
rewritten.

## Integrations

TypeScript (native, Node.js >=22.6):
  readme-assert --main ./src/index.ts

tsx:
  readme-assert --import tsx --main ./src/index.ts

SWC:
  readme-assert --import @swc-node/register/esm-register --main ./src/index.ts

esbuild:
  readme-assert --require esbuild-register --main ./src/index.ts

ts-node:
  readme-assert --require ts-node/register --main ./src/index.ts

Babel:
  readme-assert --require @babel/register --main ./src/index.js

happy-dom (browser globals):
  readme-assert --import @happy-dom/global-registrator/register.js

jsdom (browser globals):
  readme-assert -r jsdom-global/register

nock (HTTP mocking via setup file):
  readme-assert --import ./setup.js

Custom setup:
  readme-assert --require ./test-setup.js
